chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C.P.No.D-925 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objections at flag "A". 2. For orders on maintainability of main case. ------ 07.10.2025 M/s. Tufail Ahmed Aheer and Mehboob Ali Bhatti, Advocates a/w Petitioner. Mr. Munwar Ali Abbasi, Assistant Advocate General, Sindh. Mr. Ali Anwar Kandhro, Additional Prosecutor General, Sindh a/w SIP Ruknuddin Chliyani, S.H.O. P.S. A-Section, Kandhkot, ASI Kaleemullah Chandion, I.O, HC Abdul Haq Pathan and PC Ali Gohar Bahalkani, 15 Madadgar, Kandhkot. ------ Through this petition, the petitioner has prayed for the following relief(s): (a) To pass an order constituting Judicial Inquiry of incident dated 25/08/2025 by the respondents No.4 to 7 and in F.I.R. No.235/2025, under sections 324, 353, 34 P.P.C, Police Station A-Section, Kandhkot and F.I.R. No.235/2025, under section 23(a) of the Sindh Arms Act, 2013, Police Station A-Section Kandhkot. (b) To pass an order for directing the respondents No.1 to 3 to provide legal protection to the petitioner in the larger interest of justice. (c) Any other relief(s), which this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper under the circumstances. Pursuant to process issued against the respondents, official respondents No.3 to 7 have filed their respective comments, taken on record. Learned Assistant Advocate General, Sindh submits that the prayer so made is not in consonance with the provisions of law as the petitioner has remedy to approach the Secretary to the Government of Sindh, Home Department, or the Police Hierarchy regarding the prayer so made; as far as this Court is concerned, it has got no jurisdiction to order for the judicial enquiry.

Pursuant to process issued against the respondents, official respondents No.3 to 7 have filed their respective comments, taken on record. Learned Assistant Advocate General, Sindh submits that the prayer so made is not in consonance with the provisions of law as the petitioner has remedy to approach the Secretary to the Government of Sindh, Home Department, or the Police Hierarchy regarding the prayer so made; as far as this Court is concerned, it has got no jurisdiction to order for the judicial enquiry. Accordingly and in view of the above legal position, this petition is hereby dismissed. However, the petitioner is at liberty to file proper application or the case before the appropriate forum for redressal of his grievance, if so advised. Judge Judge Manzoor
